# MudKeyboard — full documentation for LLMs

> MudKeyboard is a free, open-source on-screen virtual keyboard library for Blazor and MudBlazor apps.
> The keyboard core is 100% C#/Blazor (no JavaScript); a single optional JS shim powers one feature — a
> global docked keyboard that pops up when any input is focused. It is themed automatically by MudBlazor
> (dark/light follows your theme) and is AOT- and trim-friendly. Targets .NET 8, 9 and 10.

This file is the complete documentation in plain Markdown, provided because the documentation site
(<https://mudkeyboard.pages.dev>) is a Blazor WebAssembly single-page app and therefore returns an empty
shell to a plain HTTP fetch. Everything an LLM or coding agent needs is below. A task-focused, condensed
"how to integrate" guide for agents lives at <https://mudkeyboard.pages.dev/skill.md>.

- Package: `MudKeyboard` on NuGet — <https://www.nuget.org/packages/MudKeyboard>
- Source (MIT): <https://github.com/sardar97/mudkeyboard>
- Current version documented here: **1.2.0** (.NET 8/9/10, MudBlazor 9.x)
- MudBlazor (required peer dependency): <https://mudblazor.com>

---

## Overview

MudKeyboard is built for touchscreens, kiosks, point-of-sale terminals, and any interface where a
hardware keyboard is unavailable or impractical. It provides:

- **Inline keyboards** rendered in the page and two-way bound to a string:
  - `MudKeyboard` — a full QWERTY keyboard with shift/caps and a `123`/`ABC` toggle to a numbers/symbols face.
  - `MudNumpad` — a calculator-style numeric pad, with an optional decimal point.
  - `MudPricepad` — pence-first money entry (typing `5`, `2`, `3` yields `£5.23`).
- A **global docked keyboard** (`MudKeyboardHost`) that slides up from the bottom when any input is
  focused and types at that field's caret. This is the only feature that uses JavaScript.
- A **custom-layout engine** — a layout is just rows of key tokens (data, no code).

Key properties:

- **JavaScript-free core.** Rendering, the text engine, shift/caps and the symbol toggle are pure C#/Blazor.
- **Themed by MudBlazor.** Every colour comes from MudBlazor CSS variables, so dark/light mode and your
  theme cascade automatically.
- **AOT and trim friendly.** No reflection, no dynamic code. The library sets `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>`.
- **Accessible by default.** Labelled `role="group"`, real `<button>` keys with `aria-label`, `aria-pressed`
  toggle state, `inert` while hidden, reduced-motion and safe-area support.
- **Multi-targeted.** .NET 8, 9 and 10. Inline keyboards run under Interactive Server or WebAssembly; the
  docked keyboard additionally works on static SSR pages.

---

## Requirements and hard rules

1. **MudBlazor is a required peer dependency.** MudKeyboard renders keys with MudBlazor components and
   reads MudBlazor CSS variables for colours. The app must reference `MudBlazor`, call `AddMudServices()`,
   and mount a `<MudThemeProvider />`. There is no MudBlazor-free mode. "Using MudKeyboard without MudBlazor
   components" only means binding to plain inputs/POCOs instead of MudBlazor input fields — MudBlazor is
   still required.
2. **Inline keyboards** (`MudKeyboard`, `MudNumpad`, `MudPricepad`) need an interactive render mode and do
   not require `AddMudKeyboard()`.
3. **The docked keyboard** (`MudKeyboardHost`) requires `AddMudKeyboard()` and one `<MudKeyboardHost />`.

---

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package MudKeyboard
```

```xml
<PackageReference Include="MudBlazor"   Version="9.*" />
<PackageReference Include="MudKeyboard" Version="1.*" />
```

Add namespaces to `_Imports.razor`:

```razor
@using MudKeyboard.Components
@using MudKeyboard.Models
```

Make sure MudBlazor is set up (services + providers):

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddMudServices();
```

```razor
<MudThemeProvider @bind-IsDarkMode="_isDarkMode" />
<MudPopoverProvider />
<MudDialogProvider />
<MudSnackbarProvider />
```

The docked keyboard ships two static web assets, `_content/MudKeyboard/mudKeyboard.js` and
`_content/MudKeyboard/MudKeyboard.css`; the consuming app just needs to serve static web assets normally
(`app.MapStaticAssets()` / `UseStaticFiles()` on a server, automatic for WASM standalone). Inline keyboards
need no static assets.

---

## Quickstart

### Inline full keyboard — two-way binding

```razor
<MudTextField @bind-Value="_text" Label="Name" Variant="Variant.Outlined" />
<MudKeyboard @bind-Value="_text" OnEnter="Submit" MaxLength="40" />

@code {
    private string _text = string.Empty;
    private void Submit() { /* Enter was pressed */ }
}
```

### Numpad

```razor
<MudNumpad @bind-Value="_number" AllowDecimal="true" />
@code { private string _number = string.Empty; }
```

### Pricepad (pence-first currency entry)

```razor
<MudPricepad @bind-Value="_price" CurrencySymbol="£" DecimalPlaces="2" />
@code { private string _price = string.Empty; }
```

### Custom layout

```razor
<MudKeyboard @bind-Value="_value" Variant="KeyboardVariant.Custom" Layout="MyLayout" />

@code {
    private string _value = string.Empty;

    private static readonly KeyboardLayout MyLayout = new()
    {
        Rows = new string[][]
        {
            ["m", "u", "d"],
            ["k", "e", "y"],
            [KeyTokens.Space, KeyTokens.Backspace, KeyTokens.Enter],
        },
    };
}
```

---

## Components and parameters

### `<MudKeyboard>`

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `Value` / `ValueChanged` | `string` | `""` | Two-way bindable value (`@bind-Value`). |
| `Layout` | `KeyboardLayout?` | `null` | Explicit layout; overrides `Variant`. |
| `Variant` | `KeyboardVariant` | `Full` | `Full`, `Numpad`, `Pricepad`, `Custom`. |
| `SymbolLayout` | `KeyboardLayout?` | `null` | Numbers/symbols face for the `{sym}` toggle. |
| `MaxLength` | `int?` | `null` | Optional cap on value length. |
| `Disabled` | `bool` | `false` | Disables every key. |
| `DropShadow` | `bool` | `true` | Flat keys when `false`. |
| `Sound` | `bool` | `false` | Plays a click sound on key press (pure-Blazor `<audio>`, no JavaScript). |
| `SoundSrc` | `string?` | `null` | Custom click-sound source (URL or `data:` URI); defaults to the built-in click. |
| `Palette` | `KeyboardPalette?` | `null` | Per-keyboard colour overrides. |
| `AriaLabel` | `string?` | `"On-screen keyboard"` | Accessible name for the root `role="group"`. |
| `Class` / `Style` | `string?` | `null` | Passthrough CSS. |
| `OnEnter` | `EventCallback` | — | Fires on the Enter key. |
| `OnEscape` | `EventCallback` | — | Fires on an `{esc}` key. |
| `OnInput` | `EventCallback<KeyboardInput>` | — | Emit mode: route each press elsewhere instead of editing `Value`. |

### `<MudNumpad>`

`Value`/`ValueChanged`, `AllowDecimal` (bool), `AllowNegative` (bool), `MaxLength`, `Disabled`,
`Sound` (bool), `SoundSrc` (`string?`), `OnEnter`, `Palette`, `AriaLabel` (default `"Numeric keypad"`),
`Class`, `Style`.

### `<MudPricepad>`

`Value`/`ValueChanged`, `CurrencySymbol` (default `"£"`), `DecimalPlaces` (default `2`), `AllowNegative`
(bool), `MaxLength`, `Disabled`, `Sound` (bool), `SoundSrc` (`string?`), `OnEnter`, `Palette`,
`AriaLabel` (default `"Price entry keypad"`), `Class`, `Style`.

### `<MudKeyboardHost>`

`Palette` (`KeyboardPalette?`), `Elevation` (`int`, default `8`), `MinZIndex` (`int`, default `1400`),
`Style` (`string?`), `VisibleActions`/`DisabledActions` (`KeyboardAction`), `AllowNegative` (`bool`).

New in 1.2.0 (all opt-in, default off):

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ShowValuePreview` | `bool` | `true` | Shows a bar at the top of the docked keyboard with the focused field's live value and a caret at the cursor position. Edits apply live. |
| `ShowCancel` | `bool` | `true` | Renders a **Cancel** key next to Enter that reverts to the value at focus-in and closes. While shown, the chevron-down Hide button is suppressed. |
| `CancelLabel` | `string` | `"Cancel"` | Text shown on the Cancel key. |
| `DefaultCapsLock` | `bool?` | `null` | Whether the keyboard starts caps-locked. `null` = use the global `MudKeyboardOptions.DefaultCapsLock`. |
| `ShowBackdrop` | `bool` | `false` | Dims the page behind the docked keyboard; a backdrop click cancels (reverts) and closes — unless `DisableBackdropClick` is set. |
| `DisableBackdropClick` | `bool` | `false` | Backdrop clicks no longer dismiss, requiring an explicit Enter or Cancel. Effective only with `ShowBackdrop`. |
| `Sound` | `bool` | `false` | Plays a click sound on key press (pure-Blazor `<audio>`, no JavaScript). |
| `SoundSrc` | `string?` | `null` | Custom click-sound source; defaults to the built-in click. |

The number and money keypads **replace** the field's existing value on the first digit pressed after
focus (a pre-filled `6.00` becomes the typed amount, not `6.005`); backspace, sign or a caret move resumes
editing the existing value.

### `<MudKeyboardTextField>` (`@typeparam T`)

A wrapper over `MudTextField<T>` that opts a field into the docked keyboard. Forwards common text-field
parameters (`Value`/`ValueChanged`/`ValueExpression`, `Label`, `Placeholder`, `HelperText`, `InputType`,
`Immediate`, `Disabled`, `ReadOnly`, `Variant`, `Adornment`, `AdornmentIcon`, `Lines`, `Class`, `Style`)
plus any extra attributes (such as `name`).

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `DockedKeyboard` | `bool` | `false` | Adds `data-mudkeyboard="true"`. |
| `DockedKeyboardLayout` | `string` | `""` | Forces the layout (`qwerty`, `numpad`, `decimal`, `money`). Empty = infer. |

### `<MudKeyboardNumericField>` (`@typeparam T`)

A wrapper over `MudNumericField<T>` that makes the docked keyboard show the right keypad for the field's
numeric type (`decimal` → money, `double`/`float` → decimal keypad, integers → plain numpad). Forwards
common numeric parameters (`Value`/`ValueChanged`, `For`, `Label`, `Placeholder`, `HelperText`, `Variant`,
`Margin`, `Adornment`, `AdornmentIcon`, `Immediate`, `Disabled`, `ReadOnly`, `ShrinkLabel`, `Format`,
`Min`, `Max`, `Step`, `Class`, `Style`) plus any extra attributes.

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `DockedKeyboard` | `bool` | `false` | Adds `data-mudkeyboard="true"` (required in `OptIn` mode). |
| `DockedKeyboardLayout` | `string` | `""` | Overrides the auto layout (`money`, `decimal`, `numpad`, `qwerty`). Empty = resolve from `T`. |

---

## Custom layouts and key tokens

A `KeyboardLayout` is just data:

```csharp
public sealed record KeyboardLayout
{
    public required IReadOnlyList<IReadOnlyList<string>> Rows { get; init; }
}
```

Each token is either a literal character (typed verbatim) or a brace-wrapped command. Well-known tokens
are constants on `KeyTokens`:

| Token | Constant | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `{bksp}` | `KeyTokens.Backspace` | Delete the character before the caret |
| `{enter}` | `KeyTokens.Enter` | Commit / submit (raises `OnEnter`) |
| `{space}` | `KeyTokens.Space` | Insert a space |
| `{shift}` | `KeyTokens.Shift` | One-shot shift (double-tap = caps lock) |
| `{caps}` | `KeyTokens.Caps` | Caps lock |
| `{sym}` | `KeyTokens.SymbolToggle` | Flip between letters and numbers/symbols |
| `{esc}` | `KeyTokens.Escape` | Dismiss (raises `OnEscape`) |
| `{cancel}` | `KeyTokens.Cancel` | Revert and dismiss (raises `OnCancel`); docked keyboard renders it next to Enter |

Built-in layouts on `LayoutLibrary`: `Qwerty`, `Symbols`, `Numeric`, `Numpad`, `NumpadWithDecimal`,
`Price`. Helpers: `LayoutLibrary.ForVariant(variant)` (throws for `Custom`) and
`LayoutLibrary.SymbolsForVariant(variant)` (non-null only for `Full`).

---

## Theming

The keyboard reads the ambient MudBlazor theme — toggle `MudThemeProvider`'s dark mode and every key
follows. To recolour a single keyboard, pass a `KeyboardPalette`; any slot left unset still follows the
theme.

```razor
<MudKeyboard @bind-Value="_value" Palette="Brand" />

@code {
    private static readonly KeyboardPalette Brand = new()
    {
        AccentColor = "#00897b",
        AccentTextColor = "#ffffff",
    };
}
```

| Slot | Recolours |
| --- | --- |
| `Surface` | Keyboard background |
| `KeyColor` | Literal-key background |
| `KeyTextColor` | Literal-key label |
| `AccentColor` | Accent-key (Enter / active shift) background |
| `AccentTextColor` | Accent-key label |

Values are any CSS colour — hex, `rgb()`/`hsl()`, or `var(--…)`.

---

## Emit mode (`OnInput`)

Set `OnInput` to route each press somewhere other than the keyboard's own `Value`:

```razor
<MudKeyboard OnInput="Handle" />

@code {
    private void Handle(KeyboardInput input)
    {
        switch (input.Kind)
        {
            case KeyboardInputKind.Text:      /* insert input.Text */ break;
            case KeyboardInputKind.Backspace: /* delete one char  */ break;
            case KeyboardInputKind.Enter:     /* commit           */ break;
            case KeyboardInputKind.Escape:    /* dismiss          */ break;
        }
    }
}
```

`KeyboardInput` is `readonly record struct (KeyboardInputKind Kind, string Text = "")` with factories
`KeyboardInput.Char(text)`, `.Backspace`, `.Enter`, `.Escape`. `KeyboardInputKind` is `Text`, `Backspace`,
`Enter`, `Escape`.

---

## Global docked keyboard

Register services and place one host.

```csharp
// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddMudServices();
builder.Services.AddMudKeyboard();
```

```razor
<MudThemeProvider @bind-IsDarkMode="_isDarkMode" />
<MudPopoverProvider />
<MudDialogProvider />
<MudSnackbarProvider />

<MudKeyboardHost />
```

Every editable text/number field now raises the keyboard on focus. The layout is inferred per field from
the rendered HTML: text → QWERTY, integer fields → plain numpad, floating-point fields → numpad with a
decimal point. The panel includes clear/copy/paste/cursor controls and docks one layer above the top-most
element on the page.

### Controlling which fields attach

Default is `KeyboardAttachMode.AllInputs`. Switch to opt-in:

```csharp
builder.Services.AddMudKeyboard(o => o.AttachMode = KeyboardAttachMode.OptIn);
```

| Attribute | Effect |
| --- | --- |
| `data-mudkeyboard-ignore` | Never raise the keyboard for this field (AllInputs mode). |
| `data-mudkeyboard` | Opt this field in (required in OptIn mode). |
| `data-mudkeyboard-layout="qwerty\|numpad\|decimal\|money"` | Force a layout (`price` aliases `money`). |

Set `Immediate="true"` on MudBlazor inputs so the bound value updates as keys are tapped.

### Type-aware numeric fields

`decimal`, `double` and `float` all render `inputmode="decimal"`, so the focus-capture shim cannot tell
currency from a plain decimal. `MudKeyboardNumericField<T>` resolves the keypad from the bound CLR type:

| Bound type | Docked keypad |
| --- | --- |
| `decimal` | Money — pence-first (`5·2·3 → 5.23`), like `MudPricepad`. |
| `double` / `float` | Numeric keypad with a `.` key. |
| `int`, `long`, `short`, … | Numeric keypad without a `.` key. |

```razor
<MudKeyboardNumericField @bind-Value="Model.Price" T="decimal" Format="N2"
                         Adornment="Adornment.Start"
                         AdornmentIcon="@Icons.Material.Filled.CurrencyPound" />
<MudKeyboardNumericField @bind-Value="_measure"  T="double" />
<MudKeyboardNumericField @bind-Value="_quantity" T="int" />
```

You can also force money on any field with `data-mudkeyboard-layout="money"`.

### Value preview, cancel & caps lock

`ShowValuePreview` (on by default) shows a bar at the top of the docked keyboard with the focused field's
live value and a blinking caret at the cursor position — handy when the field is hidden behind the panel,
and so caret moves (the ◂ ▸ arrows) are visible. `ShowCancel` (on by default) renders a **Cancel** key next
to Enter: Enter commits and closes, Cancel reverts to the value the field held at focus-in and closes;
rename the key with `CancelLabel`, or set `ShowCancel="false"` to drop it (which restores the chevron-down
Hide button). `DefaultCapsLock="true"` starts the keyboard caps-locked (or globally:
`AddMudKeyboard(o => o.DefaultCapsLock = true)`). `ShowBackdrop="true"` dims the page; a backdrop tap
cancels (reverts and closes) unless `DisableBackdropClick="true"`.

```razor
<MudKeyboardHost ShowValuePreview="true" ShowCancel="true" CancelLabel="Cancel" DefaultCapsLock="false" />
```

### Key click sound (opt-in, JavaScript-free)

`Sound="true"` plays a short click on every key press — a Blazor-rendered `<audio>` element (no
JavaScript), so the same parameter works on the docked keyboard and the inline `MudKeyboard` /
`MudNumpad` / `MudPricepad`. The default click is synthesised in pure C# and exposed as a `data:` URI;
set `SoundSrc` to any URL or `data:` URI to use your own. (Browsers allow sound only after the first
user interaction, so the very first tap on a fresh page may be silent.)

```razor
<MudKeyboardHost Sound="true" />
<MudNumpad @bind-Value="_qty" Sound="true" SoundSrc="/sounds/tap.mp3" />
```

---

## Render modes

| Render mode | Inline keyboards | Docked keyboard | Place `<MudKeyboardHost>` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Interactive Server | works | works | MainLayout (global mode) or App.razor outside `<Routes>` |
| Interactive WebAssembly | works | works | MainLayout |
| Interactive Auto | works | works | App.razor outside `<Routes>` with `@rendermode="InteractiveAuto"` |
| Static SSR (no circuit) | does NOT work (needs interactivity) | works | App.razor outside `<Routes>`, own render mode |
| Prerendering (any interactive mode) | safe | safe | unchanged |

### Prerendering

Safe with or without prerendering — no special handling required. The docked host only calls JavaScript in
`OnAfterRenderAsync(firstRender)`, which never runs during a server prerender pass, and it guards the call
with try/catch. Inline keyboards prerender as static HTML and wire up handlers once interactive. You do not
need `prerender: false`.

### Static SSR

Inline keyboards do not work on a static-SSR page (no circuit), but the docked keyboard does: it edits the
focused `<input>` through JavaScript on `document.activeElement`, dispatching native `input`/`change`
events so the value rides a normal form POST. Give the host its own interactive island in `App.razor`,
outside `<Routes>`:

```razor
<body>
    <Routes />
    <MudKeyboardHost @rendermode="InteractiveServer" />
    <script src="@Assets["_framework/blazor.web.js"]"></script>
</body>
```

Then opt fields in (page needs no `@rendermode`):

```razor
@page "/login"

<EditForm Model="Input" method="post" OnValidSubmit="SignIn" FormName="login">
    <MudKeyboardTextField @bind-Value="Input.Username" Label="Username"
                          DockedKeyboard="true" name="Input.Username" />
    <MudKeyboardTextField @bind-Value="Input.Password" Label="Password"
                          InputType="InputType.Password"
                          DockedKeyboard="true" DockedKeyboardLayout="qwerty"
                          name="Input.Password" />
    <MudButton ButtonType="ButtonType.Submit">Sign in</MudButton>
</EditForm>
```

A plain `MudTextField` with `data-mudkeyboard="true"` works the same way.

### Interactive Auto / WASM-rendered host

Call `AddMudKeyboard()` in both the server and the WASM client `Program.cs`.

### Mobile viewport

Add `viewport-fit=cover` to the viewport meta tag so the docked panel pads past device safe-area insets.

---

## With vs without MudBlazor input components

MudBlazor is always required. Optional is whether the fields you bind are MudBlazor inputs.

```razor
@* Inline keyboard driving a POCO string shown in a native element. *@
<input value="@_text" readonly />
<MudKeyboard @bind-Value="_text" />

@* Docked keyboard on a native input (AllInputs attaches automatically; OptIn needs the attribute). *@
<input type="text" data-mudkeyboard="true" />

@code { private string _text = string.Empty; }
```

A `<MudThemeProvider />` must still be present (the keyboard surface is rendered by MudBlazor). Missing
theme provider is the usual cause of unstyled colours.

---

## Accessibility

Each keyboard surface is a labelled `role="group"`; every key is a real `<button>` with a spoken
`aria-label` (`⌫` → "Backspace", blank space bar → "Space", `123`/`ABC` → "Numbers and symbols"/"Letters").
Shift/caps and the symbol toggle expose `aria-pressed`. The docked host is `inert` and `aria-hidden` while
hidden, its action bar is a labelled `role="toolbar"`, and focus is never stolen from the edited field. Set
the `AriaLabel` parameter (`MudKeyboard`, `MudNumpad`, `MudPricepad`) to name each keyboard. The panel
clamps to the viewport, pads safe-area insets, keeps comfortable touch targets and honours
`prefers-reduced-motion` and forced-contrast modes.

---

## AOT and trimming

The library targets `net8.0;net9.0;net10.0` and sets `<IsAotCompatible>true</IsAotCompatible>`, enabling
the trim, AOT and single-file analyzers on every build. There is no reflection or dynamic code; only
primitive strings cross the JS interop boundary, so the focus-capture shim is AOT/trim-safe too.

---

## Troubleshooting

- Inline keyboard does nothing on a static-SSR page → it needs an interactive render mode; or use the
  docked keyboard.
- Docked keyboard never appears → missing `AddMudKeyboard()`; host placed in a static-SSR layout instead of
  its own island in `App.razor`; OptIn mode without `data-mudkeyboard`; static web assets not served.
- Typed text doesn't update the bound value → add `Immediate="true"`.
- A `decimal` money field shows the plain decimal keypad → use `MudKeyboardNumericField<decimal>` or
  `data-mudkeyboard-layout="money"`.
- More than one `<MudKeyboardHost>` → place it exactly once.
- Unstyled colours → no `<MudThemeProvider />` in the tree.

---

## Links

- Docs site (WebAssembly SPA): <https://mudkeyboard.pages.dev>
- Agent skill: <https://mudkeyboard.pages.dev/skill.md>
- AI index: <https://mudkeyboard.pages.dev/llms.txt>
- NuGet: <https://www.nuget.org/packages/MudKeyboard>
- Source (MIT): <https://github.com/sardar97/mudkeyboard>
- MudBlazor: <https://mudblazor.com>
